# English L1 Standards Index

## Scope

This index covers English L1 standards integration for Cambridge Primary Path L1 and lower-primary English. It extends the Foundation English crosswalk without treating L1 textbook coverage as child progress.

## Generated Files

- Standard outcomes: `learning/curriculum/standards-integration/language-l1-standard-outcomes.jsonl`
- Crosswalk: `learning/curriculum/standards-integration/language-l1-standard-crosswalk.csv`
- Canonical objectives: `learning/curriculum/standards-integration/language-l1-canonical-objectives.yaml`
- Audit report: `docs/audit/language-l1-standards-crosswalk-report.md`

## Primary Standards Sources

| Source family | Role |
| --- | --- |
| China 2022 English | Local compulsory-education reference; Level 1 is used cautiously because its formal band is later than CPP L1 home use |
| Singapore MOE Primary English 2020 | Main lower-primary English architecture for listening/viewing, reading/viewing, speaking/representing, writing/representing, grammar, vocabulary, and early literacy |
| IB PYP Language Continuum | Developmental and inquiry-friendly observable behaviours across oral, visual, reading, and writing strands |

## Canonical Objective Lookup

| Canonical objective | Domain | Main evidence needed |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `co.eng.l1.vocabulary-context.001` | vocabulary | child recognizes, names, sorts, or uses target words with meaning visible |
| `co.eng.l1.sentence-patterns.001` | sentence patterns | child adapts a frame with meaningful substitution |
| `co.eng.l1.language-functions.001` | language functions | child completes a short communicative purpose |
| `co.eng.l1.phonics-sound-awareness.001` | phonics / sound awareness | child identifies, sorts, reads, or uses a target sound pattern |
| `co.eng.l1.listening-viewing.001` | listening / viewing | child responds to a specific oral, visual, or multimodal prompt |
| `co.eng.l1.speaking-oracy.001` | speaking | child gives a short spoken response with transcript or close paraphrase |
| `co.eng.l1.reading-strategies.001` | reading comprehension | child uses a picture, print, sequence, prediction, or meaning clue |
| `co.eng.l1.reading-fluency-readiness.001` | reading fluency readiness | child recognizes or reads familiar words or short lines with print or sound clues |
| `co.eng.l1.writing-captions-sentences.001` | writing readiness | child creates or builds a label, caption, sentence, or short description |
| `co.eng.l1.visual-multimodal.001` | visual / multimodal literacy | child connects words, pictures, symbols, or layout to meaning |
| `co.eng.l1.interaction-discussion.001` | interaction | child takes turns, asks, answers, clarifies, or gives feedback |
| `co.eng.l1.learning-habits.001` | learning habits | child leaves a dateable observation trace from a short routine |

## Foundation To L1 Continuity

Foundation English remains the entry layer: naming, short frames, listening response, simple interaction, picture-supported meaning, and cautious evidence capture.

English L1 adds:

- broader vocabulary domains
- more varied grammar and sentence patterns
- explicit phonics and sound-pattern work
- reading strategy language
- short written captions and descriptions
- more purposeful interaction and discussion routines

## Boundaries

- This is not a diagnostic pack, learning plan, worksheet, or real learning activity.
- It does not write or imply progress for Xiaoyue or Xiaoyi.
- All standards outcomes are summaries only.
- Future progress ledger entries still require real observation records.
